name: Coral Castle Full
description: The Coral Castle, located in Homestead, Florida, is an enigmatic structure built by Edward Leedskalnin. The central claim surrounding the Coral Castle is that Leedskalnin, a Latvian immigrant, single-handedly constructed the massive structure, which consists of tons of coral rock, using unknown methods that defy conventional engineering and physics. Proponents of alternative theories suggest he possessed secret knowledge of anti-gravity, magnetism, or ancient levitation techniques, possibly gleaned from lost civilizations or esoteric sources. They point to the precise alignment of the stones, the immense weight of individual blocks, and Leedskalnin's own cryptic statements as evidence. Mainstream explanations attribute the construction to Leedskalnin's ingenuity, patience, and skillful application of basic leverage principles. While the exact methods remain somewhat obscure due to Leedskalnin's secrecy, engineers and researchers have proposed plausible explanations involving winches, pulleys, and levers. The mystery surrounding the construction is enhanced by Leedskalnin's eccentric personality and the lack of eyewitnesses to his work. Skeptics argue that the 'secrets' are exaggerated and that Leedskalnin's understanding of physics, while unconventional, was not beyond the realm of known principles. The castle's construction remains an intriguing feat of engineering, but mainstream science rejects claims of anti-gravity or lost technologies. The castle now serves as a tourist attraction, fueling speculation and debate about its origins and construction methods.
subtitle: Monumental Mystery of Solitary Construction

claims.claim: Leedskalnin used anti-gravity or lost levitation techniques.
claims.evidence: The immense weight of the stones (many tons) and the fact that he seemingly worked alone suggest conventional methods are insufficient.
claims.criticism: No credible evidence of anti-gravity or lost levitation technology exists. Plausible explanations involving levers, pulleys, and winches have been proposed.

claims.claim: The Coral Castle is aligned with celestial bodies or possesses unique magnetic properties.
claims.evidence: Some researchers claim to have found alignments and unusual magnetic readings at the site.
claims.criticism: These claims are often based on selective data or misinterpretations. Any alignments are likely coincidental, and magnetic anomalies are not scientifically proven.

claims.claim: Leedskalnin possessed secret knowledge from ancient civilizations.
claims.evidence: Leedskalnin's cryptic writings and his interest in ancient cultures are interpreted as evidence of hidden knowledge.
claims.criticism: There is no concrete evidence to support the claim that Leedskalnin had access to or understood any lost knowledge. His writings are open to interpretation and do not definitively prove such knowledge.

keyFigures.name: Ray Stoner
keyFigures.role: Engineer who studied the Coral Castle's construction

mythologyConnections: Ancient Egyptian construction (Possible source of inspiration)

timeline.event: Leedskalnin begins building Rock Gate Park (original location)
Leedskalnin moves the castle to Homestead, Florida

sources.author: Rusty McClure and Jack Hebrank
sources.work: Coral Castle: The Mystery of Ed Leedskalnin and His American Stonehenge
sources.title: Rusty McClure and Jack Hebrank
